description: This figure shows X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel samples manufactured by selective laser melting (SLM) under five different laser powers, corresponding to samples P-70 (70 W), P-90 (90 W), P-110 (110 W), P-130 (130 W), and P-150 (150 W). The X-axis is 2θ ranging from 30° to 100°, and the Y-axis is diffraction intensity in arbitrary units (a.u.). All detected diffraction peaks are indexed to the face-centered cubic γ-Fe(M) austenite phase, corresponding to (111), (200), (220), (311), and (222) crystal planes, with no α-Fe(M) phase peaks observed. The full width at half maximum of the (111) peak decreases sequentially from 0.289 (P-70) to 0.237 (P-150), indicating the average grain size of SLM-fabricated samples increases with rising laser power, as higher energy input leads to slower cooling rate and longer grain growth duration. This result confirms single austenite phase is obtained for all tested SLM parameters, which benefits the strength and toughness of the low-density steel.
